title: Real-Valued Quantum Mechanics As Binary/Real Readout Constraint

internal_source.source: FLCR-04; FLCR-08; FLCR-31; FLCR-40; J3(O)/binary invariance discussion
internal_source.finding: The corpus claims that a selected complex-resolution window can admit a deterministic binary or real-valued readout. This needs a current external comparator around real-valued quantum formalism.

external_publication.title: Quantum Mechanics Based on Real Numbers: A Consistent Description

faces.correlation: The paper supplies a current peer-reviewed comparator for whether real-number structures can reproduce standard quantum predictions under a changed composition postulate. In the FLCR suite it constrains the proposed move from complex dynamics to a deterministic real/binary readout.
crystal_faces: field face: real vs complex formalism
composition face: tensor/composition rule vs FLCR adapter
observer face: readout relation vs physical postulate
falsifier face: where binary/real readout fails to reproduce complex prediction
obligations: State exactly which FLCR composition rule is being compared to the PRL real-number construction.
Separate finite binary readout claims from full quantum-theory equivalence claims.
Add a falsifier row: identify an experiment or calculation where the FLCR readout would diverge from standard complex quantum mechanics.
Use this pair as a required comparator before promoting J3(O)/binary invariance into 41-80 physics papers.

validation_review.next_action: Attach package to routed papers, then convert obligations into paper-local claim rows and workbook exercises.
